title: Directors and Chief Executives
rationale: AI will compress analysis, planning, and coordination layers around this role, but final accountability and organizational leadership remain human-intensive.

title: Other Department Managers
rationale: The work contains a large digital coordination component, but execution still depends on human supervision, escalation handling, and relationships.

title: Computing Professionals
rationale: This is one of the most exposed occupation groups because core tasks are digital, decomposable, and already seeing large productivity gains from AI.

title: Architects, Engineers and Related Professionals
rationale: AI can speed up analysis and documentation substantially, but engineering work still carries physical-world, safety, and regulatory constraints.

title: Health Professionals (except nursing)
rationale: AI will be highly useful in the information-processing parts of the job, but core care decisions and patient-facing work remain only moderately exposed.

title: Nursing Professionals
rationale: Nursing is only moderately exposed because AI helps the paperwork and monitoring layers, not the physical and interpersonal core of the occupation.

title: Other Teaching Professionals
rationale: AI will reshape content creation and assessment heavily, but live teaching, student management, and trust still require human presence.

title: Business Professionals
rationale: Core outputs are text, spreadsheets, and analysis, so AI can automate or compress a significant portion of daily work.

title: Computer Associate Professionals
rationale: The role is not as exposed as software engineering, but a large share of repetitive troubleshooting and documentation is automatable.

title: Secretaries and Keyboard-Operating Clerks
rationale: The occupation is highly exposed because much of the work is routine digital coordination and document handling.

title: Numerical Clerks
rationale: Routine numerical processing and document-driven bookkeeping are among the most compressible office workflows.

title: Client Information Clerks
rationale: A large part of first-line customer communication is structured and scriptable, making this group highly exposed.

title: House Keeping and Restaurant Services Workers
rationale: The administrative layer can be digitized, but the core service work still depends on physical presence and human interaction.

title: Personal Care Workers
rationale: This group has low exposure because the value comes from physical, relational, and continuous human care.

title: Shop Salespersons and Demonstrators
rationale: Digital selling tools and automated recommendations will compress parts of the role, but a large in-person component remains.

title: Market Gardeners and Crop Growers
rationale: AI can guide crop decisions but cannot cheaply replace large amounts of physical farm work in Indian conditions.

title: Market-Oriented Animal Producers and Related Workers
rationale: The occupation remains low exposure because the physical care tasks dominate and the economics favor augmentation over replacement.

title: Market-Oriented Crop and Animal Producers
rationale: AI will mainly augment planning and advisory layers while leaving the core production work largely intact.

title: Building Frame and Related Trades Workers
rationale: The work is highly physical and site-specific, so AI exposure remains low.

title: Building Finishers and Related Trades Workers
rationale: AI affects estimation and supervision more than the hands-on finishing work, so exposure stays low to moderate.

title: Machinery Mechanics and Fitters
rationale: AI will improve diagnosis and maintenance planning, but the occupation still depends heavily on physical repair work.

title: Electrical and Electronic Equipment Mechanics and Fitters
rationale: Compared with purely mechanical trades, the digital diagnostic layer is larger, but the occupation still cannot be done entirely from a computer.

title: Textile, Garment and Related Trades Workers
rationale: The work is repetitive enough to face automation pressure, but labor-cost economics and physical handling keep exposure moderate.

title: Food and Related Products Machine Operators
rationale: Exposure is moderate because plant automation can advance, but the work still includes manual materials handling and shift-floor response.

title: Motor Vehicle Drivers
rationale: AI will change routing and dispatch systems a lot, but the actual driving task remains only moderately exposed in India.

title: Sales and Service Elementary Occupations
rationale: This group remains low exposure because much of the work is informal, physical, and tightly linked to local presence.

title: Agricultural, Fishery and Related Labourers
rationale: The work is overwhelmingly manual, local, and low-cost, so AI exposure is minimal.

title: Labourers in Mining, Construction, Manufacturing and Transport
rationale: AI changes coordination around the job more than the core labor itself, so direct exposure remains low.
